title: SALB MSE Trial Specifications Document
text:
Preface
This is the Trial Specifications Document (TSD) for the International Commission for the Conservation of Atlantic Tunas (ICCAT) Southern Atlantic albacore Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE).

title: 1  Introduction
text:
1.1 MSE Framework
This document describes the trial specifications for the Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE) of South Atlantic albacore tuna (Thunnus alalunga), undertaken for the International Commission for the Conservation of Atlantic Tunas (ICCAT).
This is a living document and will be updated as the MSE process progresses.
The MSE is built on the openMSE framework (MSEtool v4+). All functions and code for reproducing the operating models, management procedures, and performance indicators are contained in the iccatSALB R package and its associated GitHub repository. The analysis itself is carried out through a set of scripts that call on these functions, which are also available in the repository alongside the package.

title: 1  Introduction
text:
1.2 Stock Assessment
The operating models are conditioned on the ICCAT South Atlantic albacore stock assessment, fitted using Stock Synthesis 3 (SS3) v3.30.23.1 (Methot and Wetzel 2013), referred to here as the S05 model run.

TBD: link the assessment report once published


1.2.1 Data
The S05 assessment is fit to catch and abundance index data from 1956–2024, comprising:

5 catch fleets (Fleet_01–Fleet_05)
5 CPUE/abundance indices:

Indx_CTP-LL_TB1 (Chinese Taipei longline, time block 1)
Indx_CTP-LL_TB2 (Chinese Taipei longline, time block 2)
Indx_JPN-LL_TB2 (Japan longline)
Indx_BR_URY-LL (Brazil/Uruguay longline)
Indx_ZAF-BB (South Africa baitboat)




1.2.2 Model Structure

Model years: 1956–2024 (69 years)
Areas: 1 (single area, no movement)
Sexes: 1 (combined-sex model)
Ages: 0–15, with age 15 as the plus group

title: 2  Operating Models
text:
2.1 Reference Set
The operating models (OMs) are conditioned using the S05 assessment model (see Section 1.2) as a base case.
The S05 assessment model is referred to as the Reference OM.
The Reference Set OMs are currently designed as a factorial uncertainty grid with two axes of uncertainty:
Additional operating models may be added to the Reference OMs - e.g., uncertainty in the steepness parameter of the Beverton-Holt stock recruit relationship.
Each axis is represented at three levels, corresponding to the 25th, 50th, and 75th percentiles of the estimated parameter values. The 50th percentile level for each axis matches the S05 reference assessment, so the combination of the two 50th-percentile levels corresponds to the Reference OM.
This produces a 3 x 3 uncertainty grid with 9 reference operating models.
The von Bertalanffy growth parameters at each percentile level are shown in Table 2.1.
Each level for natural mortality is defined by a reference Lorenzen M value (at reference age 11), which determines the full age-specific M vector via the Lorenzen relationship.
Because this relationship scales with weight-at-age, the resulting M-at-age vector also depends on growth, Table 2.2 shows the vector at the 50th-percentile of the growth parameters (see Table 2.1) only. The vectors for the 25th- and 75th-percentile growth scenarios differ slightly from these values.

Figure 2.2 shows the historical spawning biomass depletion (\(SB/SB_0\)) trajectory for each of the 9 reference grid OMs, while Figure 2.3 shows the spawning biomass relative to the MSY level.

title: 2  Operating Models
text:
2.2 Robustness OMs
Robustness OMs have not yet been developed for SALB.
Potential Robustness OMs may include:

uncertainty in TAC implementation (e.g., overages in TAC)
impacts of climate change (e.g., time-varying life-history parameters)
alternative recruitment scenarios.

title: 3  Management Procedures
text: Candidate management procedures are in development.

title: 4  Performance Metrics
text: The performance metrics are in development.
